events.notes: Patient presents with 103.2F fever, tachycardia (115 bpm), and localized pain in lower extremities. Vital signs meet 2/3 qSOFA criteria for sepsis.
ER Resident notes suspicion of infection. Orders blood cultures and broad-spectrum antibiotics (Vancomycin).
No administration of ordered antibiotics. No follow-up nursing notes for 13.5 hours. Vital signs not re-checked.
Patient found unresponsive, hypotensive (70/40), progressing to septic shock.
Necrosis identified in both feet due to prolonged septic shock and vasopressor use.
Bilateral below-knee amputation performed due to non-viable tissue.

summary: The 14-hour delay in administering broad-spectrum antibiotics despite clear sepsis indicators at triage is a definitive deviation from the Standard of Care (Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines), directly resulting in septic shock and subsequent double amputation.
